titleStmt.title: 246. Information Memorandum From the President’s Special Assistant (Rostow) to President ) to President Johnson

div.head: 246. Information Memorandum From the President’s Special Assistant (Rostow) to President Johnson Source: Johnson Library, National Security File, Country File, Korea—Pueblo Incident, Vol. I, Part B (through January). Confidential.
div.p: Secretary Rusk asked Joe Sisco to come and talk to me about the following:

list.item: The Secretary General, through Ralph Bunche, has suggested that the Security Council, by means of SC “consensus,” call on Thant to provide good offices. This same proposal has been transmitted by the Secretary General to the USSR simultaneously through Nesterenko. Goldberg is unaware how much prior discussion, if any, the Secretary General has had with the Soviets.

Thant proposes that the attached “consensus”Not printed. be adopted by the Security Council. As you note, the Secretary General would “be invited to take the initiative and offer his good offices in whatever manner he may consider appropriate and helpful.” This would be said in the context of reducing the prevailing tension and finding an acceptable solution. If the consensus is adopted, the Secretary General would send Deputy Foreign Minister of Rumania, Mircea Malitza, to Pyongyang. Thant has already discussed this proposal with the Rumanians who are agreeable to sending Malitza on this mission.

div.p: You may wish to discuss this matter with Secretary Rusk on the telephone and consider it further at tomorrow’s lunch.
